generator: web-curated from SEAB/MOE syllabi (6091/6092/6093/4052/5076/5087/5088) and Primary Science 2023

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: 6093 Pure Biology — four themes, 14 topics
chunks.content: Theme I Cells and Chemistry of Life: (1) Cell structure and organisation — animal/plant cells, specialised cells; (2) Movement of substances — diffusion, osmosis, active transport; (3) Biological molecules — food tests, enzymes, denaturation. Theme II Human Body: (4) Nutrition; (5) Transport; (6) Respiration; (7) Excretion; (8) Homeostasis and coordination; (9) Infectious diseases. Theme III Living Together: (10) Plant nutrition and transport; (11) Ecology and environment. Theme IV Continuity of Life: (12) Molecular genetics; (13) Reproduction; (14) Inheritance.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topics 1–2: Cell structure and movement of substances
chunks.content: Identify organelles: nucleus, cytoplasm, cell membrane, mitochondria, ribosomes; plant cell wall and chloroplast. Compare specialised cells (red blood cell, root hair, sperm). Explain diffusion down concentration gradient; osmosis as water movement across partially permeable membrane; active transport against gradient using energy. Practical: potato cylinders in sucrose solutions.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topic 3: Biological molecules and enzymes
chunks.content: Food tests: starch (iodine), reducing sugar (Benedict's), protein (biuret), fats (ethanol emulsion). Enzymes as biological catalysts; lock-and-key model; effect of temperature and pH on enzyme activity; denaturation. Carbohydrates, proteins and fats as building blocks and energy sources.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topic 4: Nutrition in humans
chunks.content: Balanced diet and deficiency diseases (scurvy, rickets, anaemia). Alimentary canal: mouth, oesophagus, stomach, small intestine, large intestine; roles of amylase, protease, lipase; bile emulsifies fats. Absorption via villi; assimilation. Mechanical vs chemical digestion.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topic 5: Transport in humans
chunks.content: Double circulation; heart chambers and valves; coronary arteries. Blood components: red cells (haemoglobin), white cells, platelets, plasma. Arteries, veins, capillaries structure and function. Tissue fluid and lymphatic system overview.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topics 6–7: Respiration and excretion
chunks.content: Aerobic respiration releases energy from glucose; word equation glucose + oxygen → carbon dioxide + water. Anaerobic respiration in muscles (lactic acid) and yeast (ethanol). Gas exchange at alveoli; diaphragm and rib cage. Kidney: nephron filtration, selective reabsorption, urine formation; urea from deamination.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topics 8–9: Homeostasis and infectious diseases
chunks.content: Negative feedback for blood glucose (insulin, glucagon) and body temperature. Nervous system: reflex arc; endocrine glands and hormones. Pathogens: bacteria, viruses, fungi; transmission (air, water, contact, vectors); antibiotics vs antivirals; vaccination and herd immunity.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topics 10–11: Plants and ecology
chunks.content: Photosynthesis: 6CO2 + 6H2O → C6H12O6 + 6O2; limiting factors (light, CO2, temperature). Leaf structure; xylem and phloem; transpiration pull. Food chains and webs; trophic levels (~10% energy transfer); carbon cycle; deforestation and pollution impacts.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: Topics 12–14: Genetics, reproduction, inheritance
chunks.content: DNA double helix; genes code for proteins; base pairing A-T, G-C. Mitosis vs meiosis; sexual and asexual reproduction; human reproductive hormones. Monohybrid and dihybrid crosses; Punnett squares; sex linkage; pedigree charts. Dominant, recessive, homozygous, heterozygous, phenotype, genotype.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4052 E-Math — three strands
chunks.content: Strand N Number and Algebra: primes, indices, surds, polynomials, quadratics, inequalities, graphs, functions. Strand G Geometry and Measurement: congruence, similarity, Pythagoras, trigonometry, mensuration, coordinate geometry, vectors. Strand S Statistics and Probability: data handling, averages, diagrams, probability, tree diagrams.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: E-Math Number and Algebra highlights
chunks.content: Factorise quadratics; complete the square; solve quadratic equations by formula and graphically. Simultaneous linear equations; linear inequalities. Algebraic fractions; change of subject. Sketch quadratic graphs; discriminant and nature of roots.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: E-Math trigonometry and mensuration
chunks.content: sin, cos, tan in right triangles; angles of elevation and depression; bearings from North. Sine and cosine rules for any triangle. Arc length l = rθ and sector area A = ½r²θ (θ in radians where required). Volume and surface area of sphere, cone, pyramid, composite solids.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: E-Math statistics and probability
chunks.content: Mean, median, mode for grouped data; histograms and frequency polygons. Cumulative frequency curves; median and quartiles; box-and-whisker plots. Standard deviation to compare spreads. Probability of single and combined events; tree diagrams; mutually exclusive and independent events.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary 3 Science topics (2023 syllabus)
chunks.content: Diversity of living and non-living things: MRS GREN characteristics; classification of plants and animals. Diversity of materials: wood, metal, plastic properties. Life cycles in plants and animals (e.g. butterfly, frog). Interaction of forces: magnets — poles, attraction/repulsion, magnetic materials.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary 4 Science topics (2023 syllabus)
chunks.content: Plant system: parts and functions (roots, stem, leaves). Human system: digestive system organs and functions. Cycles in matter and water: states of matter, mass and volume. Energy: light (shadows, reflection) and heat (conduction, convection, radiation).

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary 5 Science topics (2023 syllabus)
chunks.content: Reproduction in plants and animals; water cycle in depth. Plant respiratory and circulatory systems (gas exchange, transport). Human respiratory and circulatory systems (lungs, heart, blood vessels). Electrical system: simple circuits, conductors, insulators, switches, bulbs.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary 6 Science topics (2023 syllabus)
chunks.content: Photosynthesis and energy conversion in plants. Interaction of forces: friction, gravity, elastic spring force; balanced vs unbalanced forces. Interactions within the environment: food chains/webs, adaptations, human impact. PSLE integrates all five themes spiral coverage.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: 5087 Combined Science (Physics + Biology)
chunks.content: Combined Science papers test subsets of pure Physics (6091) and Biology (6093) at reduced breadth and depth. 5086 = Physics + Chemistry; 5087 = Physics + Biology; 5088 = Chemistry + Biology. Practical assessment integrates inquiry skills across sections. Use pure science disciplinary ideas but expect shorter explanations.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 Mechanics — kinematics to energy
chunks.content: Kinematics: speed, velocity, acceleration; distance-time and velocity-time graphs; equations of motion for uniform acceleration. Dynamics: Newton's laws; free-body diagrams; friction and terminal velocity. Turning effects: moment = force × perpendicular distance; centre of gravity; stability. Pressure: p = F/A; liquid pressure p = hρg. Energy: work, kinetic and potential energy, power, efficiency.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 Electromagnetism and induction
chunks.content: Magnetic field around wire and solenoid; Fleming's left-hand rule for motor effect. Electromagnetic induction: moving conductor in field induces emf; dynamo and transformer principles (qualitative). AC generator vs DC motor applications.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Lower Secondary Science — bridging to O-Level
chunks.content: Sec 1–2 integrated science: scientific method, measurement, cells and systems, matter and particles, forces and energy, diversity of living things. Builds inquiry skills before subject specialisation in Sec 3 for Pure/Combined Sciences. Emphasis on practical work, data analysis, and ethics in science.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Lower Secondary 16 topics (2021 syllabus)
chunks.content: Themes: Scientific Endeavour, Diversity, Models, Interactions, Systems. Topics: (2) physical properties of matter (3) chemical composition (4) separation techniques (5) ray model of light (6) model of cells (7) particulate nature (8) atoms and molecules (9) forces and energy transfer (10) heat transfer (11) chemical changes (12) ecosystems (13) electrical systems (14) digestive system (15) transport in living things (16) human reproductive system.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4049 Additional Mathematics — three strands
chunks.content: Algebra: A1 quadratic functions, A2 equations and inequalities, A3 surds, A4 polynomials and partial fractions, A5 binomial expansions, A6 exponential and logarithmic functions. Geometry and Trigonometry: G1 trig functions identities equations, G2 coordinate geometry in 2D, G3 proofs in plane geometry. Calculus: C1 differentiation and integration with applications to gradients, areas and kinematics.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4049 Calculus — differentiation and integration
chunks.content: Differentiation: derivative as gradient and rate of change; product, quotient, chain rules; stationary points, tangents and normals. Integration: indefinite and definite integrals; area under curve and between curves; kinematics linking displacement, velocity, acceleration via calculus.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4049 Trigonometry — identities and equations
chunks.content: Six trigonometric functions for any magnitude (degrees and radians). Graphs of sin, cos, tan; amplitude and period. Identities: Pythagorean, addition, double-angle. Solve trigonometric equations in given intervals. Proofs using trigonometric identities.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4052 E-Math — 18 syllabus topics
chunks.content: Number and Algebra: N1 numbers and operations, N2 ratio and proportion, N3 percentage, N4 rate and speed, N5 algebraic expressions and formulae, N6 functions and graphs, N7 equations and inequalities, N8 set language, N9 matrices. Geometry: G1 angles/polygons, G2 congruence and similarity, G3 circle properties, G4 Pythagoras and trigonometry, G5 mensuration, G6 coordinate geometry, G7 vectors. Statistics: S1 data handling, S2 probability.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: 4052 G3 Properties of circles
chunks.content: Symmetry: equal chords equidistant from centre; perpendicular bisector of chord through centre; tangents from external point equal; line from external point to centre bisects angle between tangents. Angles: angle in semicircle is 90°; tangent perpendicular to radius; angle at centre twice angle at circumference; angles in same segment equal; opposite segments supplementary.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 Topics 7–12 Thermal and Waves
chunks.content: Topic 7 Kinetic particle model: states of matter, gas pressure, Brownian motion. Topic 8 Thermal processes: conduction, convection, radiation. Topic 9 Thermal properties: specific heat capacity Q=mcΔT, latent heat Q=mL. Topic 10 General wave properties: v=fλ, transverse and longitudinal. Topic 11 EM spectrum order and applications. Topic 12 Light: reflection, refraction, lens ray diagrams.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 Topics 13–19 Electricity and Magnetism
chunks.content: Topic 13 Static electricity: charging, hazards. Topic 14 Current: Q=It, conventional current. Topic 15 DC circuits: Ohm's law, series/parallel, combined resistance. Topic 16 Practical electricity: P=IV, E=Pt, fuses, earthing. Topic 17 Magnetism: field lines, electromagnet. Topic 18 Electromagnetism: motor effect F=BIL. Topic 19 Induction: generator, transformer (qualitative).

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 Topic 6 Energy stores framework (2024)
chunks.content: Energy stored in kinetic, gravitational, elastic and thermal (internal) stores. Transferred by mechanical work, electrical heating, radiation. Conservation of energy; efficiency calculations. Sankey diagrams for energy flow. Removed from 2024 syllabus: detailed temperature measurement with thermometers; vernier calipers emphasis reduced.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 6092 Chemistry — all 12 content areas
chunks.content: Section I: (1) Experimental chemistry (2) Particulate nature including atomic structure (3) Bonding and structure. Section II: (4) Chemical calculations (5) Acid-base (6) Qualitative analysis (7) Redox (8) Periodic table patterns (9) Energetics (10) Rate of reaction. Section III: (11) Organic chemistry (12) Air quality.

chunks.subject: Chemistry
chunks.title: 6092 Organic chemistry detail
chunks.content: Homologous series: alkanes CnH2n+2, alkenes CnH2n, alcohols, carboxylic acids, esters. Functional groups determine properties. Reactions: combustion, addition to alkenes, fermentation to ethanol, esterification. Polymers: addition vs condensation; environmental issues of plastics.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: 5086 Combined Science (Physics + Chemistry)
chunks.content: 5086 tests Physics and Chemistry subsets at O-Level combined standard. Papers integrate MCQ, structured and practical assessment. Topics span mechanics, electricity, particulate matter, acids, rates, and qualitative analysis at reduced depth compared to pure sciences 6091/6092.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: 5088 Combined Science (Chemistry + Biology)
chunks.content: 5088 combines Chemistry and Biology disciplinary ideas. Links biological molecules and food tests with chemistry; ecology with environmental chemistry. Practical tests skills from both sections; shorter explanations than pure 6092/6093.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: Primary Math P1–P3 strands
chunks.content: Strands: Number and Algebra, Measurement and Geometry, Statistics. P1: numbers to 100, addition/subtraction, length, 2D shapes. P2: numbers to 1000, multiplication/division intro, mass, volume, time. P3: numbers to 10 000, fractions, area and perimeter, bar graphs.

chunks.subject: Mathematics
chunks.title: Primary Math P4–P6 and PSLE
chunks.content: P4: factors, multiples, fractions, decimals, symmetry, angles. P5: ratio, percentage, rate, algebra intro, volume, average. P6: circles (area/circumference), speed, pie charts, special quadrilaterals, PSLE heuristics (draw model, guess-check, work backwards). 2026 PSLE uses 2021 syllabus for P6.

chunks.subject: Science
chunks.title: Primary Science 2023 — fixed topics per level
chunks.content: From 2023 each level has fixed topics (not upper/lower block). P3: diversity living/non-living, materials, life cycles, magnets. P4: plant/human systems, matter, light, heat. P5: reproduction, water, respiratory/circulatory systems, electricity. P6: photosynthesis, energy conversion, forces, environment interactions. PSLE 2026 first cohort on new syllabus.

chunks.subject: Biology
chunks.title: 6093 all 14 topics checklist
chunks.content: 1 Cell structure 2 Movement of substances 3 Biological molecules 4 Nutrition humans 5 Transport humans 6 Respiration humans 7 Excretion humans 8 Homeostasis and coordination 9 Infectious diseases 10 Plant nutrition and transport 11 Ecology 12 Molecular genetics 13 Reproduction 14 Inheritance. Three papers: MCQ, structured, practical.

chunks.subject: Physics
chunks.title: 6091 all 20 topics checklist
chunks.content: I Measurement: 1 Physical quantities. II Mechanics: 2 Kinematics 3 Dynamics 4 Turning effects 5 Pressure 6 Energy. III Thermal: 7 Kinetic model 8 Thermal processes 9 Thermal properties. IV Waves: 10 General waves 11 EM spectrum 12 Light. V E&M: 13 Static 14 Current 15 DC circuits 16 Practical electricity 17 Magnetism 18 Electromagnetism 19 Induction. VI: 20 Radioactivity.
